questionHtml: By writing x4-9x3+24x2+22x-12 as a product of one linear and one cubic factor, prove that the equation has at least one complex root.
markschemeHtml:
attempt to express as a product of a linear and cubic factor           M1

x+1x3-10x2+34x-12          A1A1

 

Note: Award A1 for each factor. Award at most A1A0 if not written as a product.

 

since for the cubic, p2<3q 100<102          R1

there is at least one complex root          AG

 

[4 marks]

meta.title: By writing x4-9x3+24x2+22x-12 as a product of one linear and one cubic factor, prove that the equation has at least one complex root.
